title: Move product admin to an independent strangler app
context: The built-in Medusa dashboard remains available as a fallback during the strangler migration, but the product admin UX is moving to an independent Next app that calls Medusa through a same-origin BFF. The legacy backend admin extension still depends on @medusajs/ui and React 18 types, while the storefront and new admin app use Next 16 and React 19.
decision: Create apps/admin as the new product admin surface, keep Medusa as a headless /admin/* API engine, and route browser traffic through the Next admin BFF with httpOnly admin JWT cookies. Default CI typechecking covers backend API code, storefront, and apps/admin. The legacy Medusa dashboard extension typecheck remains visible as typecheck:legacy-admin but is no longer part of the default typecheck/check:ci path because that surface is being strangled and carries the known @medusajs/ui React 18 boundary.
consequences: New admin work must land in apps/admin and use the BFF for /admin/* calls.
Browser code must not call Medusa directly or receive the admin JWT.
Legacy dashboard regressions remain observable through typecheck:legacy-admin while migration is in progress, but they do not block the new admin CI path.
